title: Macht in den internationalen Beziehungen
further_info: Recommended Reading Einstiegslektüre: Arabi, Kasper 2025. Power and International Relations. Princeton, NJ: PUP. Barnett, Michael/Duvall, Raymond 2005. Power in International Politics, in: International Organization 59(1): 39-75. Berenskoetter, Felix/Williams, Michael J. (Hrsg.) 2007. Power in World Politics. London: Routledge. Bueno De Mesquita, Bruce 2022. The Invention of Power: Popes, Kings, and the Birth of the West. New York: Public Affairs. Drezner, Daniel 2021. Power and International Relations: A temporal view, in: European Journal of International Relations 27(1): 29-52. Greene, Robert 2026. Power. Die 48 Gesetze der Macht. München: Hanser. Guzzini, Stefano 2005. The Concept of Power, in: Millennium 33(3): 495-522. Heine, Jorge/Fortin, Carlos/Ominami, Carlos 2025. The Non-Aligned World: Striking Out in an Era of Great Power Competition. Cambridge: Polity Kang, David C. et al. 2025. What Does China Want? in: International Security 50(1): 46-81. Katzenstein, Peter J. 2017. Protean Power. Cambridge: CUP. Lukes, Steven 2005 [1974]. Power: A Radical View, 2. Auflage. Houndsmills: Palgrave. Meierding, Emily/Sigman, Rachel 2021. Understanding the Mechanisms of International Influence in an Era of Great Power Competition, in: Journal of Global Security Studies 6(4): 1-18. Nye, Joseph S. Jr. 2011. Macht im 21. Jahrhundert. München: Siedler. Ohnesorge, Hendrik W. 2020. Soft Power. Wiesbaden: Springer VS. Perthes, Volker 2026. Die Multipolarisierung der Welt. Ein geopolitischer Wegweiser. Berlin: Suhrkamp. Popitz, Heinrich 1992. Phänomene der Macht. Autorität – Herrschaft – Gewalt – Technik, 2. Auflage. Tübingen: J.C.B. Mohr. Rodriguez-Triocci, Emilio 2024: What about the BRICS? Examining power politics in a changing world order, in: Journal of Political Power 17(1): 21-41.
